text.title: User Story Mapping — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of User Story Mapping must enforce.

rule.statement: The map starts from the user journey backbone (horizontal); stories hang from journey steps.
rule.rationale: Feature-first maps lose user perspective and produce backlogs prioritised by team convenience.

rule.statement: The map names the walking-skeleton release: smallest end-to-end usable path.
rule.rationale: Without a named walking skeleton, teams build features in parallel and miss the integration risk.

rule.statement: The map names the MVP release: smallest valuable path users will accept.
rule.rationale: Without a named MVP, scope drifts and 'launch' keeps slipping.

rule.statement: Every story carries acceptance criteria (≥3 per story).
rule.rationale: AC-less stories let developers and BAs imagine different definitions of done.

rule.statement: Every story references the use case (or journey step) it implements.
rule.rationale: Lost traceability means downstream changes orphan stories.
